entries.summary: Phase 3 completed: 12 P1 High Priority Configuration & Infrastructure tasks. Fixed version bump regex, model naming (Qwen3-1.7B), env var validation, BM25 optimization, CI workflow issues, CORS configuration, and subprocess reliability. All tasks passed QA gates.

metadata.plan_id: QA Findings Remediation — Document Q&A Assistant

top_rejection_reasons: BM25 exception handling incomplete - needed to check all handler sites
Model name confusion - clarified that Qwen3-1.7B is correct per user
lessons_learned: Pydantic BaseSettings provides robust env var validation
BM25 batch optimization requires careful parameter design
CI workflows need explicit failure modes - no silent passing
User direction overrides plan when conflicts arise

entries.summary: Phase 3: Validation, synthesis, and delivery of the enhancement report. Critic validated 141 raw findings down to 53 confirmed (20 HIGH, 33 MEDIUM), rejecting 88 (62% rejection rate). Final report synthesized with 54 validated findings across 10 categories, implementation roadmap, and codebase strengths. Architect verification confirmed report completeness, no source file modifications, and independence from prior QA audit.
top_rejection_reasons: 62% of findings were rejected — primary reasons: assumed web capabilities (CSS, ARIA) in desktop app, referenced non-existent code, stylistic preferences, already handled by existing code
lessons_learned: Gate enforcement blocks read-only analysis tasks (2.0, 2.5, 2.6) — reviewer/test_engineer gates are designed for code-writing tasks, not analysis delegations. Future read-only plans should use TIER 0 classification.
Critic validation is essential for enhancement reviews — 62% rejection rate caught 7 findings referencing non-existent code and many UI/UX findings that assumed web capabilities in a desktop app.

Cascading dependencies between findings (e.g., ARCH-8 enables QUAL-1 and RES-12) should be explicitly mapped in the report — the dependency map produced by the critic was valuable for the implementation roadmap.
Serial delegation is slower but produces higher-quality findings than parallel batching — the designer produced more accurate desktop-specific UI analysis when given focused single-pass prompts.

entries.summary: Phase 3 completed all 5 tasks: SettingsDialog offline cleanup (3.1-3.2), keyboard shortcuts + button styling (3.3), progress bar + thinking animation + WM_DELETE_WINDOW (3.4), accessibility improvements (3.5). Council review found 1 HIGH issue: _is_operation_active missing from _initialize_engine() — fixed. All tests pass (43+ Phase 3 tests).
top_rejection_reasons: Missing _is_operation_active flag management in _initialize_engine() — found during Phase 3 council review (not during individual task review)
Test failures from _make_button refactor breaking source-code assertions in test_keyboard_shortcuts_styling.py
lessons_learned: _is_operation_active flag must be set in ALL background thread functions, not just user-facing ones — _initialize_engine() was missed because it runs at startup
When refactoring CTkButton calls to a helper function (_make_button), existing source-code-searching tests break because the text= kwarg becomes a positional arg
tkinter singleton prevents running multiple test files that create CTk windows in the same pytest invocation — run per-file
Task 3.2 test failure from prior session (_classify_error ollama references) required a separate fix delegation before the task could be marked complete
The reviewer correctly identified the _initialize_engine gap during the full council review — individual task reviewers missed it because each task was reviewed in isolation
